comment_text.sn9.1:3.1: This verse is particularly tricky; see Bodhi’s note in Connected Discourses for discussion and other interpretations. | Pajahāsi and bhavāsi are reflexive second person singular imperative (= pajaha, bhava).
comment_text.sn9.1:3.2: Sārayāmase is reflexive first person plural imperative causative (= sārayāma). The deity is using the “royal we”. | I take taṁ as accusative (“you”) and sataṁ as accusative of purpose (“for the purpose of being mindful”).

root_text.sn9.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ aññataro bhikkhu kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn9.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena so bhikkhu divāvihāragato pāpake akusale vitakke vitakketi gehanissite.
root_text.sn9.1:1.4: Atha kho yā tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe adhivatthā devatā tassa bhikkhuno anukampikā atthakāmā taṁ bhikkhuṁ saṁvejetukāmā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn9.1:5.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu tāya devatāya saṁvejito saṁvegamāpādīti.

translation_text.sn9.1:1.2: At one time one of the mendicants was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn9.1:1.3: Now at that time that mendicant, during their day’s meditation, was thinking bad, unskillful thoughts to do with the domestic life.
translation_text.sn9.1:1.4: The deity haunting that forest had sympathy for that mendicant, and wanted what’s best for them. So they approached that mendicant wanting to stir them up, and addressed them in verse:
translation_text.sn9.1:2.2: yet your mind strays to outward things.
translation_text.sn9.1:2.3: As a person, dispel the desire for people;
translation_text.sn9.1:2.4: then you’ll be happy, free of greed.
translation_text.sn9.1:3.2: let us remind you to be mindful.
translation_text.sn9.1:3.3: The dusty abyss is so hard to cross;
translation_text.sn9.1:3.4: don’t let sensual dust drag you down.
translation_text.sn9.1:4.1: Just as a bird strewn with dirt
translation_text.sn9.1:4.2: sheds that clingy dust with a shake;
translation_text.sn9.1:4.3: so too, an energetic, mindful mendicant
translation_text.sn9.1:4.4: sheds that clingy dust with a shake.”
translation_text.sn9.1:5.1: Impelled by that deity, that mendicant was struck with a sense of urgency.

variant_text.sn9.1:4.1: paṁsukunthito → paṁsukuṇḍito (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); paṁsuguṇṭhito (pts2ed); paṁsukuṇṭhito (mr)
